title: Firstmac Limited — Consumer harm
investigation_objective: The Federal Court found Firstmac Limited contravened section 994E(3) of the Corporations Act by failing to take reasonable steps to ensure distribution of its 'High Livez' investment product to hundreds of term deposit customers, between October 2021 and September 2022, was consistent with its target market determination. Firstmac was ordered to pay $8 million plus ASIC's costs. This was the first contested-trial finding of contravention under Australia's design and distribution obligations regime.
scope: Found by the Federal Court to have contravened design and distribution obligations following a contested trial - the first such finding under the regime; Ordered to pay an $8 million penalty plus costs; Distributed an investment product to term deposit customers in a manner found inconsistent with its own target market determination
